creator: cyberbotx@cyberbotx.com
text:
When using RealVNC's vnc.so extension with the latest Xorg 7.3 upgrade, the X server will crash with a signal 11 upon a remote computer connecting (although it does attempt to show the display before crashing).  Rebuilding net/vnc after the Xorg upgrade doesn't help.  The following is my Xorg.0.log file up to the point of the crash. Also, the crash doesn't seem to happen until some type of input happens, be it mouse or keyboard input.

Fatal server error:
Caught signal 11.  Server aborting

Fix: 

Unknown, I suspect it might be an issue that should be brought up with the RealVNC developers, but I want to see if there was a FreeBSD fix or not first.
How-To-Repeat: - Upgrade to Xorg 7.3
- Install net/vnc
- Activate the vnc.so extension by adding the following line to your xorg.conf file in /etc/X11 under the "Module" Section:
Load "vnc"
- Launch Xorg
- Attempt to connect to the VNC server from another computer
- Send some input, resulting in the signal 11 crash
